arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with brettsm/vsftpd2.3.4-backdoor-exploit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with boudarbiss/RAT-STA (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bitgodhack/AndroidHack_BackDoor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [web, shell_windows, shell_linux] in the Command and Control category. Fits when the engagement needs: XWorm C2 Absolute Path Traversal Vulnerability.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: XWorm C2 Absolute Path Traversal Vulnerability.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install pycryptodome. XWorm C2 Absolute Path Traversal Vulnerability.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Command and Control, use xworm-c2-path-traversal because XWorm C2 Absolute Path Traversal Vulnerability.
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use xworm-c2-path-traversal as a cataloged Command and Control tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Command and Control tools, pair xworm-c2-path-traversal with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `xworm-c2-path-traversal --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
